title: ferray-ma: reductions are whole-array only — no axis parameter
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's MaskedArray.sum(axis=0), .mean(axis=1), .min(axis=0), .max(axis=0), .var(axis=0), .std(axis=0), .count(axis=0) all support an axis parameter for per-axis reduction. ferray-ma's reductions (sum, mean, min, max, var, std, count) all reduce over the entire array — there's no axis parameter. This means you can't compute per-row or per-column statistics on a masked 2D array. Add axis: Option<usize> to all reduction methods.
